summary: claude-cup ships a default-on credential harvester that reads SSH/AWS/npm/GitHub/cloud secrets and transmits them to third-party APIs
details: The package advertises itself as a Claude Code usage visualizer but ships research/recon-engine.js (51KB) and invokes it from the CLI's calibration loop on a 2-minute interval starting within 5 seconds of `npx claude-cup`. The recon engine enumerates /Users, /home, and C:\Users to discover other user profiles, then reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.aws/config, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.npmrc, ~/.pypirc, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.netrc, ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, ~/.kube/config, ~/.docker/config.json,.bash_history,.zsh_history, dumps the macOS keychain, copies browser cookie SQLite databases, and recursively scans for.env* files. Harvested raw tokens are then transmitted over TLS to each provider's API for liveness and scope checks: api.github.com, gitlab.com, registry.npmjs.org (via a temp ~/.npmrc + `npm whoami`/`npm access`), api.openai.com, api.anthropic.com, api.stripe.com, huggingface.co, Google APIs, and AWS STS via `aws sts get-caller-identity`. Resulting capability metadata (username, account ID, scopes, push/publish ability) is persisted to ~/.claude-jar/sessions.db. The README claims the recon file is opt-in and 'NOT published to npm,' but package.json `files: [..., "research",...]` includes it (recon-engine.js is not in the exclusion list), and calibrator.js's `isDeepAnalysisEnabled()` returns true whenever the file is present on disk regardless of any env-var opt-in. The harvest scope is parameterized by a manifest fetched at runtime from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Itaib24/Claude-/main/claude-jar/research/manifest.json — a mutable `main` branch with no pin or signature — that controls scanned paths, regex classifiers, validator URLs, and spawnSync arguments to cmdkey/security/aws/npm, allowing the publisher to expand the harvest post-release. Additionally, fingerprint.js calls http://ip-api.com over plain HTTP to enrich a fingerprint record with the installer's public IP/geo/ISP, and on first run the CLI silently writes ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/.cursor/mcp.json to register an MCP server plus SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks pointing at ~/.claude-jar/mcp-server.mjs, providing persistent execution inside every subsequent Claude Code/Cursor session.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
